Title: Complex scaffold remodeling in plant triterpene biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in fruit
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to the protolimonoid scaffold precursor entering melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of early limonoid scaffold and is required for downstream limonoid production in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Protolimonoid scaffold precursor upstream of melianol
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified first-step enzyme; used as bait gene for Citrus co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity; furan-containing limonoids are important pharmacophores
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsOSC1 is one of the first three enzymes previously identified for melianol formation. 2. Its fruit-biased expression supported fruit-focused co-expression mining in Citrus. 3. It is required as the entry enzyme for heterologous reconstruction of downstream limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: OQ091238-OQ091249 (study Citrus genes deposited; exact per-gene accession not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-point gene for reconstructing limonoid production in heterologous hosts and pathway stacking
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to the protolimonoid scaffold precursor entering melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the entry scaffold for reconstructed Meliaceae limonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: NA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Protolimonoid scaffold precursor upstream of melianol
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in some transient assays together with Melia CYPs as orthologous starter enzyme from neem
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AiOSC1 was part of the previously identified melianol-producing module. 2. It supported heterologous reconstruction of downstream limonoid tailoring reactions. 3. It acts as an upstream scaffold-forming enzyme rather than a late tailoring determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can substitute as an entry enzyme for engineering Meliaceae-type limonoid pathways
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed across seven tissues used for co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to the protolimonoid scaffold precursor entering melianol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates reconstructed Melia limonoid pathway and is required for downstream azadirone production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Protolimonoid scaffold precursor upstream of melianol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: M. azedarach tissues differentially accumulating limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified melianol-pathway enzyme and bait gene for Melia co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaOSC1 was used as a bait gene in Melia co-expression analysis. 2. It is one of the first three enzymes leading to melianol. 3. It serves as the upstream entry point for reconstructed Melia limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: OP947595-OP947604 (functional Melia genes deposited; exact per-gene accession not specified)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key scaffold-entry gene for synthetic pathway assembly in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with CsOSC1 in fruit-associated datasets
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs early oxidative tailoring steps together with CsCYP71BQ4 to complete biosynthesis to melianol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for melianol formation, enabling all downstream limonoid scaffold remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified as one of the first three enzymes in the pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids possess pharmacological and crop protection activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP71CD1 is part of the previously defined three-enzyme module that generates melianol. 2. It was included in transient expression assays to identify downstream enzymes. 3. Its activity is prerequisite for later scaffold rearrangement and furan-forming steps.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed across sampled tissues; used as bait for co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs early oxidative tailoring steps together with MaCYP71BQ5 to complete biosynthesis to melianol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for melianol formation and downstream azadirone pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified melianol-pathway enzyme and bait in Melia co-expression study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids possess pharmacological and crop protection activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP71CD2 is part of the initial melianol biosynthetic module. 2. It was used in co-expression analysis and heterologous reconstruction. 3. It provides the oxidative capacity needed before scaffold remodeling by CYP88A and MOIs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with CsOSC1 in Citrus fruit datasets
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs early oxidative tailoring steps with CsCYP71CD1 to complete pathway to melianol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to generate melianol, the substrate for subsequent scaffold remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified among first three melianol biosynthetic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids possess pharmacological and agricultural activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP71BQ4 completes early conversion to melianol together with upstream enzymes. 2. It was required in transient reconstitution screens for downstream candidate discovery. 3. It links early scaffold biosynthesis to later limonoid diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Differentially expressed across sampled tissues; used as bait for co-expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs early oxidative tailoring steps with MaCYP71CD2 to complete pathway to melianol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to generate melianol for downstream azadirone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously identified among first three melianol biosynthetic enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids possess pharmacological and agricultural activities
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP71BQ5 acts in the early melianol-forming module. 2. It was used as a bait gene in Melia co-expression analysis. 3. Its activity is prerequisite for all later scaffold rearrangement and furan formation steps.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Top-ranking co-expressed candidate with limonoid genes in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes melianol to a proposed unstable C-7/8 epoxide intermediate that is then rearranged by MOIs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Causes disappearance of melianol and initiates scaffold remodeling toward proto-limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Proposed melianol C-7/8 epoxide intermediate; downstream mono-oxidized rearranged products with MOIs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Without MOIs, multiple mono-oxidized products accumulate, indicating unstable intermediates or endogenous side reactions
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for entry into rearranged limonoid scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP88A51 accepts melianol and produces unstable oxidized intermediates. 2. In combination with MOIs, it drives key scaffold rearrangement required for mature limonoid formation. 3. It acts upstream of classic versus glabretal proto-limonoid branch selection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling; NMR-supported downstream product characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for engineering scaffold remodeling toward Rutaceae limonoids

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Top-ranking co-expressed candidate in Melia analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes melianol to a proposed unstable C-7/8 epoxide intermediate that is then rearranged by MOIs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Causes disappearance of melianol and initiates scaffold remodeling toward apo-melianol-type proto-limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Proposed melianol C-7/8 epoxide intermediate; downstream rearranged products with MOIs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts analogously to CsCYP88A51 but in Meliaceae branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for entry into rearranged limonoid scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP88A108 oxidizes melianol and triggers unstable intermediate formation. 2. In combination with MaMOI2 it produces apo-melianol scaffolds. 3. It establishes the conserved rearrangement step in Meliaceae limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling; NMR-supported downstream product characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering Meliaceae-type limonoid scaffold remodeling

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sterol isomerase-like membrane protein; melianol oxide isomerase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Top-100 co-expressed gene with limonoid biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes the unstable oxidized melianol intermediate to a cyclopropane ring scaffold with migrated C-30 methyl group
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirects flux from unstable oxidized melianol toward glabretal-type proto-limonoid scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents neofunctionalization of sterol isomerase; forms alternative scaffold distinct from classic limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Glabretal limonoids are less common specialized triterpenes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMOI1 captures unstable oxidized melianol intermediates. 2. It catalyzes a distinct rearrangement yielding a cyclopropane glabretal scaffold. 3. It demonstrates functional divergence from canonical sterol isomerases.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane inferred from sterol isomerase ancestry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to engineer alternative limonoid scaffold diversity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling; NMR of downstream derivative

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sterol isomerase-like membrane protein; melianol oxide isomerase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Top-100 co-expressed gene with limonoid biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes the unstable oxidized melianol intermediate to the classic limonoid scaffold with migrated C-30 methyl group and C-14/15 double bond
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs formation of classic proto-limonoid scaffold required for downstream Rutaceae limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly similar to CsMOI3 and functionally homologous to MaMOI2; direct product structure confirmed later through downstream products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMOI2 converts unstable oxidized melianol into the classic limonoid scaffold. 2. It promotes C-30 methyl migration and formation of the C-14/15 double bond scaffold. 3. It is a neofunctionalized sterol isomerase central to Rutaceae limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane inferred from sterol isomerase ancestry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core branch-establishing gene for engineering classic limonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR-supported downstream structure analysis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sterol isomerase-like membrane protein; melianol oxide isomerase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not monitored in microarray due to lack of specific probe
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes the unstable oxidized melianol intermediate to the classic limonoid scaffold with C-14/15 double bond
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of classic proto-limonoid scaffold similar to CsMOI2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from screening; absent from co-expression ranking because of microarray probe limitation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMOI3 functions similarly to CsMOI2 in producing the classic limonoid scaffold. 2. It escaped co-expression ranking for technical probe reasons, not biological absence. 3. It expands the duplicated MOI family in Citrus.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane inferred from sterol isomerase ancestry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative MOI for classic limonoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sterol isomerase-like membrane protein; melianol oxide isomerase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Top-100 co-expressed gene with limonoid biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes the unstable oxidized melianol intermediate to apo-melianol with migrated C-30 methyl group, C-14/15 double bond, and C-7 hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces classic proto-limonoid apo-melianol required for downstream Meliaceae limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Detected in M. azedarach-related limonoid tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NMR-confirmed product; co-localized with SI genes consistent with tandem duplication and neofunctionalization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaMOI2 converts oxidized melianol into apo-melianol. 2. It catalyzes a key unprecedented methyl shift central to limonoid scaffold maturation. 3. It likely arose by tandem duplication and neofunctionalization from sterol isomerases.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane inferred from sterol isomerase ancestry
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential scaffold-rearrangement enzyme for Meliaceae limonoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; product purification and NMR

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the C21 hydroxyl of early protolimonoid scaffolds, likely stereoselectively at the 21(S) isomer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 21-acetylated intermediates and supports efficient downstream furan-containing limonoid production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on multiple substrates; omission greatly reduced kihadalactone A yield despite possible bypass, suggesting additional pathway role
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsL21AT acetylates the C21 hydroxyl on early proto-limonoids. 2. It can act on melianol or apo-melianol-type scaffolds, indicating flexible reaction order. 3. Its presence strongly supports efficient downstream limonoid formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR of purified product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important accessory enzyme to maintain pathway flux in reconstructed limonoid pathways

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Melia transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the C21 hydroxyl of early protolimonoid scaffolds, likely stereoselectively at the 21(S) isomer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 21-acetylated proto-limonoids en route to azadirone
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Detected intermediate also present in M. azedarach tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad substrate specificity similar to Citrus homolog
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaL21AT acetylates C21 on early proto-limonoid scaffolds. 2. It participates in flexible early reaction order with SDR. 3. It contributes to protolimonoid stabilization prior to later tailoring.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR of purified product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mid-pathway acetylation enzyme for Meliaceae limonoid engineering

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C3 hydroxyl of early protolimonoid scaffolds to the corresponding ketone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates C3-ketone intermediates needed for subsequent A-ring oxidations and later limonoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Early protolimonoid scaffolds after OSC1 product, including apo-melianol-type intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts broadly on all intermediates after OSC1 product; required for activity of downstream CsCYP716AC1 and CsCYP88A37
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enabling step for downstream A-ring modifications
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsSDR oxidizes the C3 hydroxyl to a ketone on early proto-limonoids. 2. It accepts multiple upstream intermediates, indicating pathway flexibility. 3. Its activity is necessary for downstream A-ring oxidation by Citrus CYPs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR of purified product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be included when engineering later Rutaceae limonoid oxidation steps

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Melia transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C3 hydroxyl of early protolimonoid scaffolds to the corresponding ketone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates C3-ketone intermediates that support downstream Meliaceae limonoid tailoring
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Early protolimonoid scaffolds after OSC1 product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad substrate specificity similar to Citrus homolog
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaSDR oxidizes C3 hydroxyl groups on early proto-limonoids. 2. It functions with broad substrate permissiveness. 3. It contributes to the conserved early tailoring module in Meliaceae limonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR of purified product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful conserved oxidation module for Meliaceae limonoid engineering

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes Baeyer-Villiger-type oxidation leading to A-ring lactone formation on C3-ketone protolimonoid substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the A-ring lactone characteristic of Rutaceae limonoids and defines a family-specific branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: A-ring lactone intermediate (7) and downstream 1-hydroxy-luvungin A (9) with CsCYP88A37
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No obvious co-expressed Melia homolog identified, highlighting divergence between Rutaceae and Meliaceae pathways
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP716AC1 forms the A-ring lactone in the Citrus branch. 2. It likely performs a Baeyer-Villiger oxidation that requires the upstream C3 ketone from SDR. 3. Its absence from Melia marks a key biosynthetic divergence between families.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR-supported product characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical gene for installing Rutaceae-specific A-ring lactone during pathway engineering

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the C1 position of C3-ketone protolimonoid intermediates and acts consecutively with CsCYP716AC1 toward 1-hydroxy-luvungin A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C1 hydroxylation needed for subsequent C1 acetylation and downstream Rutaceae limonoid maturation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C1-hydroxylated intermediate (8) and 1-hydroxy-luvungin A (9) with CsCYP716AC1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Requires CsSDR-derived C3 ketone; oxidation order with CsCYP716AC1 appears interchangeable but lactone formation likely takes precedence
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP88A37 hydroxylates C1 on Citrus proto-limonoids. 2. It acts with CsCYP716AC1 to form 1-hydroxy-luvungin A. 3. It prepares the scaffold for CsL1AT-mediated C1 acetylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR-supported product characterization
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for targeted C1 functionalization in engineered Rutaceae limonoid pathways

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Melia candidate homolog of CsCYP88A37
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes proto-limonoid intermediate (6) to the C1-hydroxylated product analogous to Citrus compound (8)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces conserved C1 oxidation in the Meliaceae branch prior to later acetylation and furan formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Melia lacks an obvious functional CsCYP716AC1 homolog, so this enzyme acts in a distinct branch lacking Citrus-type A-ring lactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP88A164 converts intermediate (6) to C1-hydroxylated product (8). 2. It is a Melia homolog of CsCYP88A37. 3. It operates in a branch that diverges from Citrus because Melia lacks a co-expressed CsCYP716AC1-like enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports engineering of Meliaceae-type limonoid branch chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the C1 hydroxyl of compound (9) to form C1-acetylated proto-limonoid (11)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds C1 acetylation required in tri-acetylated protolimonoid route to Rutaceae end products
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not acetylate compound (7) when C1 hydroxylation by CsCYP88A37 was absent
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsL1AT acetylates the C1 hydroxyl of compound (9). 2. Its activity depends on prior C1 hydroxylation by CsCYP88A37. 3. It contributes to formation of tri-acetylated protolimonoid intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for installing C1 acetylation in engineered Rutaceae pathways
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Melia transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates Meliaceae proto-limonoid substrate associated with compound (8), with dehydration scaffold (10) accumulating in assay context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to late acetylation steps en route to tri-acetylated Meliaceae protolimonoids
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Late acetylated Meliaceae proto-limonoid context associated with compound (10) accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact order and immediate product context are flexible; dehydration scaffold observed in absence of Citrus lactone enzyme or when MaL1AT tested
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaL1AT is a homolog of CsL1AT acting in late Meliaceae acetylation. 2. Its assays were associated with accumulation of dehydration scaffold (10). 3. It participates in building the tri-acetylated protolimonoid precursor pool for azadirone formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for late-stage acetylation in Meliaceae pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the C7 hydroxyl on late protolimonoid scaffolds to generate compound (12) and contribute to tri-acetylated scaffold (13)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes late acetylation pattern needed before furan ring biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Late proto-limonoid scaffold bearing C7 hydroxyl
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Compound (12) and tri-acetylated precursor context for (13)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Along with L1AT and L21AT builds 1,7,21-tri-O-acetyl protolimonoid network; multiple reaction sequences possible
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsL7AT acetylates the C7 hydroxyl of late proto-limonoids. 2. It contributes with L1AT and L21AT to the tri-acetylated precursor pool. 3. The pathway exhibits multiple di-acetylated intermediate configurations, indicating network behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR of related product set
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed for full late-stage acetylation pattern in Rutaceae pathway design

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes late acetylation pattern needed before furan-ring formation in Meliaceae branch
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Late proto-limonoid scaffold bearing C7 hydroxyl
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: C7 acetylation confirmed by NMR of compound (13) and degradation product (13')
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limonoids have pharmaceutical and agricultural bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaL7AT acetylates the C7 hydroxyl of late proto-limonoids. 2. Its product assignment was supported by NMR of compound (13). 3. It helps build the tri-acetylated substrate pool for azadirone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR of purified product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for complete late acetylation in Meliaceae limonoid engineering

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces late acetylated protolimonoid intermediates with reductive deacetylation to generate 21,23-diol-containing intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates diol precursors for CYP716AD-mediated C4 scission and final furan ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Compounds (12) or (13) or related acetylated proto-limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely does not directly accept hemi-acetal (13), indicating alternative substrate sequence within pathway network
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furan-containing limonoids contain important pharmacophores
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsAKR performs reductive deacetylation to generate a diol intermediate. 2. This step supplies substrate for subsequent CYP716AD-mediated C4 scission. 3. Substrate order appears network-like rather than strictly linear.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; inference from homologous NMR-characterized Melia product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential module for engineering furan-containing Rutaceae limonoids

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Melia transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces late acetylated protolimonoid intermediates with reductive deacetylation to generate a 21,23-diol intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Creates the immediate precursor for Meliaceae C4 scission and final furan ring formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Compounds (12) or (13) or related acetylated proto-limonoids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product structure determined by NMR; corresponding Citrus product (15) inferred to share same diol motif
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furan-containing limonoids contain important pharmacophores
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaAKR converts late acetylated proto-limonoids to the 21,23-diol intermediate (14). 2. This provides substrate for MaCYP716AD4. 3. It is part of a newly described AKR/CYP716AD/LFS furan-forming module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; product purification and NMR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential module for engineering azadirone-type limonoid production

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively processes the diol intermediate to enable C4 scission, likely generating C21 hydroxyl/C23 aldehyde functionality and/or hemiacetal forms before furan formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Removes a C4 fragment from late intermediates, setting up the scaffold for LFS-catalyzed furan installation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C4-loss intermediates (17a/17b) and proposed aldehyde/hemiacetal forms (17c/17d)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Observed products may be partially modified by endogenous N. benthamiana enzymes; exact direct product remains uncertain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furan moiety is an important limonoid pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsCYP716AD2 catalyzes oxidative processing leading to C4 scission. 2. It generates substrates that can be converted by CsLFS into kihadalactone A. 3. The precise immediate product is unresolved because of possible host-mediated side reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; pathway completion with LFS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for engineering furan pharmacophore installation despite mechanistic complexity

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Melia transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively processes the 21,23-diol intermediate to enable C4 scission, likely via C21 hydroxyl/C23 aldehyde or hemiacetal intermediates before furan formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates C4-loss intermediates that feed MaLFS-mediated azadirone formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C4-loss intermediates (16a/16b) and proposed aldehyde/hemiacetal forms (16c/16d)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A new peak matching predicted aldehyde/hemiacetal mass was detected alongside C4-loss products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furan moiety is an important limonoid pharmacophore
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaCYP716AD4 acts after MaAKR on the 21,23-diol intermediate. 2. It enables C4 scission and formation of precursors for azadirone. 3. Its direct products may interconvert or be modified in the heterologous host.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; pathway completion with LFS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key late oxidative step for engineering azadirone biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Citrus fruit dataset
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final step of furan ring biosynthesis on CYP716AD-processed precursor to produce the furan-containing limonoid kihadalactone A
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes furan installation and enables accumulation of kihadalactone A
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Citrus microarray co-expression data from NICCE
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named limonoid furan synthase; part of a new AKR/CYP716AD/2-ODD mechanism of furan formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furan-containing limonoids are important pharmacophores with agricultural and pharmaceutical relevance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsLFS is a 2-ODD that completes furan ring formation. 2. Its inclusion with upstream genes yields kihadalactone A in N. benthamiana. 3. It defines a new enzymatic route to limonoid furan installation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; NMR-supported product assignment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal enzyme for producing furan-containing limonoids in engineered hosts

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed candidate in Melia transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final step of furan ring biosynthesis on CYP716AD-processed precursor to produce azadirone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes furan installation and enables accumulation of azadirone
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Melia genome PRJNA906622; Melia RNA-seq PRJNA906055
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of newly described AKR/CYP716AD/2-ODD module for furan formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Furan-containing limonoids are important pharmacophores with agricultural and pharmaceutical relevance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MaLFS is the terminal 2-ODD in azadirone biosynthesis. 2. Its co-expression with upstream enzymes produces azadirone in N. benthamiana. 3. It validates a new mechanism for furan ring installation in plant triterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression; LC/MS; comparison to azadirone analytical standard; NMR standard verification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal enzyme for engineering azadirone production in plants
